# Overnight log — 2026-06-07 (machine: desktop · crane-desk)

**Mission:** Add **Plaits** as Parallax's second synth engine (authentic MI firmware → WASM, mirroring the Braids port), and build the multi-engine plumbing the app has never exercised before (engine registry, engine picker, engine-aware Explain/ModelPicker/bindings). Keep the scope, Explain panel, and sequencer grid working throughout.

**Why Plaits, not Edges (the original ask):** Pre-flight found Edges can't be faithfully ported — its signature square voices are generated by *AVR hardware timers* (no software DSP exists to lift), and its one software oscillator is tangled in AVR assembly + PROGMEM tables. User chose Plaits instead (the backlog's recommended flagship): clean portable C++ with a proper `Voice::Render()`, same toolchain as Braids.

**Ground truth from source (fresh clone of pichenettes/eurorack):**
- `plaits::Voice::Render(Patch&, Modulations&, Frame{short out; short aux;}*, size)` — int16 out, **48 kHz**, 24-sample blocks.
- 24 engines (16 classic `engine/` + 6 `engine2/`: chiptune, phase-distortion, 6-op FM, string-machine, VA-with-VCF, wave-terrain).
- Patch controls: engine, note, harmonics, timbre, morph, decay, lpg_colour. Internal LPG + decay envelope built in.
- stmlib is shared across MI modules → most already vendored; refresh only what's missing (HysteresisQuantizer2 etc.).

**Verification caveat:** I can build WASM (emsdk present) and verify *structurally* (renders non-zero, correct pitch via FFT, no NaN) but CANNOT ear-test (AudioContext needs a real user tap). User must ear-confirm Plaits in the morning.

**Working style:** commit + push each sub-step (auto-push authorized per global prefs; on `main` per project convention). Defer genuine blockers to "Waiting on you" rather than forcing.

---

### Phase C — Plaits worklet + engine + data ✓ DONE
- [x] C1. `public/plaits-worklet.js` — 48 kHz, **12-sample** blocks (kBlockSize timing), gate scheduling with forced re-strike edge. (`90f8968`)
- [x] C2. `src/audio/engines/PlaitsEngine.ts` — no JS gain envelope (Plaits' LPG shapes amplitude); GainNode = master level only. Registered. (`90f8968`)

### Phase D — Verify + wrap ✓ DONE

- [x] D2. This log + PENDING-DECISIONS + NEXT-STEPS finalized; pushed.

---

## Done overnight

**Plaits is live as Parallax's second engine — pick it from the new Engine selector above the model picker.** Everything the user asked to keep (scope, Explain panel, sequencer grid/staff) works with it unchanged. Plain-language summary:

1. **Edges turned out to be the wrong module to port** (pre-flight finding, surfaced before the run): its signature square-wave sound is made by AVR *hardware timers*, not software, so there's literally no DSP code to lift. You chose Plaits instead — the right call, and the backlog's recommended flagship. (Plaits even includes a **Chiptune** engine that scratches the Edges itch.)

2. **Built the multi-engine plumbing the app never had** (Phase A). A new engine registry (`src/audio/registry.ts`) is now the one place that knows what engines exist. The model picker and Explain panel read from it per active engine, so they adapt to any engine's models/knobs with no per-engine code. The Explain panel now renders **one card per macro knob** — Braids shows Timbre/Color, Plaits shows Harmonics/Timbre/Morph. A new **Engine picker** hot-swaps engines; the melody and transport keep playing across a swap.

3. **Ported Plaits authentically** (Phases B–C). Vendored Émilie Gillet's MIT Plaits DSP, wrote a WASM shim + worklet mirroring the Braids approach, exactly as authentic as Braids — no hand-rolled oscillators. **All 24 synthesis models** (the 16 classics + the 8 from firmware 1.2: virtual-analog-VCF, phase distortion, 6-op FM ×3 banks, wave terrain, string machine, chiptune), each with hand-written "what this knob does" Explain text quoted from the official manual.

4. **Verified end-to-end without ears** (I can't ear-test from the harness). The WASM produces correct, pitched, non-silent audio for every one of the 24 engines (Node test); in the live browser the analyser shows real signal on note-on and a decaying release tail on note-off, no drone at rest; swapping back to Braids still works. Type-check clean throughout.

**Commits (all pushed to `main`, deploys auto):** `40108ec` registry refactor · `9899e4a` engine picker · `295cc54` vendor Plaits · `9bdd7ff` shim+WASM · `90f8968` worklet+engine+data · `b42554e` dev hook.

## Waiting on you

**No blockers — the whole feature shipped.** Two things for you, both optional:

1. **Ear-confirm Plaits on the live site (the one thing I couldn't do).** Switch the Engine selector to **Plaits**, scroll the 24 models, and play. I proved signal *flows* and the pitch is right, but not that each model *sounds* the way it should to your ear. Listen especially to the **drums (BD/SD/HH)** and **6-op FM banks** — see "good to know" below.

2. **Optional tuning, once you've listened** (all are exposed knobs, not code changes):
   - **DECAY / LPG TONE** knobs (new for Plaits) set how long/bright a note rings. Defaults are 0.5; if notes feel too short or too plucky for sustained sequences, raise DECAY.
   - **Default Plaits model** is engine 0 (VAF, "Classic Waveshapes + Filter"). Say if you'd prefer it open on something else.

**Good to know (faithful Plaits behaviour, not bugs):**
- The **3 "6-Op FM" entries** are the DX7 banks; you scroll the 32 presets *within* each bank using the **HARMONICS** knob, not the model picker (that's how Plaits works).
- The **drum models** (and plucky string/FM tones) are percussive one-shots — they ring out per DECAY regardless of note length, exactly like on hardware.
- Note timing is quantised to the audio render block (~2.7 ms) — inaudible for the sequencer; noted as a future refinement if ultra-tight retriggers ever matter.
- A **dev-only** `window.__parallax` handle was added for harness verification — it's stripped from production builds, so it ships nothing.
